q: Which correctly distinguishes an ecosystem carbon sink from a carbon source?
options: A carbon sink releases more CO2 than it absorbs; a carbon source absorbs more than it releases
A carbon sink accumulates more carbon than it releases (net absorption); a carbon source releases more carbon than it absorbs (net emission)
A sink and source differ only in location: ocean sinks are polar; terrestrial sources are tropical
Carbon sinks only apply to geological storage; living ecosystems are always carbon sources
explanation: Old-growth forests and healthy oceans are sinks; degraded peatlands and deforested areas often become sources, releasing stored carbon as CO2 or methane.
